Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query to filter an event log table event log based on case length Specifically Keep cases with 6 events discard cases with 6 events Columns case id activity timestamp Output All columns for the qualifying events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Standard SQL approach 1 Aggregate to find case sizes 2 Filter case ids where count 6 3 Join back to original table or use window function IN EXISTS subquery

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: DuckDB supports window functions CTEs and semi joins efficiently

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Option 1 Window Function often very fast in DuckDB due to vectorized execution

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: DuckDB optimizes IN subqueries to semi joins mark joins usually making it very efficient

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For filter groups by aggregate the QUALIFY clause supported in DuckDB is the most idiomatic and concise modern SQL way

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is the cleanest most DuckDB native way It handles the window function filtering automatically without a subquery
